web.title: 2026 Average Cost of Kitchen Cabinets - HomeAdvisor
web.description: Professional kitchen cabinet installation costs between $100 and $1,200 per linear foot depending on whether you choose stock, semi-custom, or custom designs.
web.markdown:
# Average Kitchen Cabinet Costs

Kitchen cabinets range widely from **$100 to $1,200 per linear foot.** A typical 10-by-10-foot kitchen would run anywhere from **$2,000 to $24,000**, though most fall in the range of **$4,000 to $13,000** **.** You can expect to get anywhere from **65% to 80% return on your cabinet and kitchen remodel investment** when you sell your home.

How we get this data

Request Project Quote

Written byHomeAdvisor

Staff Writer

Reviewed by [Cati O'Keefe](https://www.homeadvisor.com/r/about-cati-okeefe/)

Expert Reviewer

Updated Jun 17, 2026

Cost Overview:

- Professional kitchen cabinet installation costs between **$100 and $1,200 per linear foot** depending on whether you choose stock, semi-custom, or custom designs.

- Your material selection and cabinet construction type will determine your final project total, with options ranging from affordable laminate to premium stainless steel.

- You should also account for the cost of professional measuring services, appliance relocation, and potential permit fees when creating your budget.

- [Hiring a local cabinet professional](https://www.homeadvisor.com/near-me/cabinet-contractors/) ensures your kitchen project is done well and boosts the overall home resale value.


Get quotes from up to 3 pros!

Enter a zip below and get matched to top-rated pros near you.

![Geo Icon](https://res.cloudinary.com/angi-prod/image/upload/v1748027941/location.svg)

Find Local Pros

Cabinets help define the look and usability of your kitchen. If you have a dated or difficult-to-use design, your kitchen could be falling short of its full potential as the entertainment and meal-prepping focal point of your home. Installing a new design will improve its looks and usability - and boost the appeal of your home. Here's a comprehensive guide on design, costs and materials.

## Kitchen Cabinet Prices Per Linear Foot

The linear foot pricing of **$100 to $1,200** includes everything from off-the-shelf to high-end custom options. The construction breaks down into these three categories, each with its own price point and specific set of pros and cons:

| Type | Price Per Linear Foot | Price Per Unit |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Stock | $100 - $300 | $200 - $650+ |
| Semi-Custom | $150 - $650 | $250 - $800+ |
| Custom | $500 - $1,200 | $500+ |

### Stock Cabinets

Stock cabinets are the most affordable option available to homeowners at **$100 to $300 per linear foot installed,** or **$50 to $100 per linear foot for cabinets alone.** Stock or builder’s-grade construction comes with fewer design options, but costs considerably less than semi-custom and custom alternatives.

Quality and measurement issues are sometimes a problem with prefab types. Be sure to carefully measure the location of your new install before making any purchases. If you're unsure, have a [cabinet professional](https://www.homeadvisor.com/emc.Cabinets-directory.-12009.html) measure your space.

- **Advantage:** Affordable price.

- **Disadvantage:** Lack of design options, occasional measurement and quality issues.


### Semi-Custom Cabinets Cost

If you have a larger budget or need custom sizing or aesthetics, semi-custom designs offer a wider variety of options at **$150 to $650 per linear foot installed.** The materials alone cost **$75 to $400 per linear foot**, or **$150 or more per unit.** Semi-stock is perfect for homeowners looking for unique, long-lasting types without the hefty price of custom designs.

- **Advantage:** Reasonable price, more design and measurement options.

- **Disadvantage:** Lack of exotic designs and materials.


### Custom Cabinets

[Custom cabinets cost](https://www.homeadvisor.com/cost/cabinets-and-countertops/custom-cabinets/) **$500 to $1,200 per linear foot installed**. Materials make up between **$300 and $750 per linear foot**. They’re perfect for oddly-shaped kitchens or specific design and functionality needs. While custom installs meet your exact specifications, you can expect a substantial cost increase.

- **Advantage:** Endless design possibilities.

- **Disadvantage:** Price.


### Plywood vs. MDF vs. Solid Wood

**Plywood with Solid Wood:** Most high-end semi-custom and many custom installations use a combination of plywood and solid wood. The cabinet boxes use plywood with real wood veneers for the boxes and solid wood or plywood with veneers for doors and drawers. Manufacturers use real wood for structural supports and sometimes the cabinet faces.

**MDF:** Most stock and some lower-end semi-custom varieties use medium density fiberboard (MDF), also called particleboard, with a thin plastic veneer applied to the top.

**Solid Wood:** It is rare to find any custom installation that is 100% solid wood. High-end plywood tends to hold up better to use, doesn’t warp in moist environments and has a higher mechanical strength than solid wood. Once applied, real wood veneer looks, feels and functions as well as or better than solid wood.

## New Kitchen Cabinet Cost Calculator by Material

It's important to also consider the mass appeal of your materials. Exotic designs and materials could potentially hurt the resale value of your home. The materials you choose depends on your budget and design. Here's a breakdown of the most popular materials:

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Wood Veneer | $2,000 - $15,000+ |
| Laminate | $4,000 - $15,000 |
| Wood | $5,000 - $25,000+ |
| Acrylic | $5,000 - $20,000 |
| Stainless Steel | $25,000 - $38,000 |

### Cabinet Wood Types Price Comparison

Wood ranges from **$5,000 to $25,000 or more.** You’ll only pay an average of **$5 per linear foot** difference between varieties, so choose the one that best complements your home design. Here's a quick look at a variety of sought-after wood types:

- **Maple** has a straight, tight grain that makes it a perfect candidate for both traditional and contemporary designs. It is durable and accepts paints and stains well.

- **Cherry** is a heavier wood, characterized by its straight, close grain. Many homeowners choose it for its elegance and natural durability. You can use it in both traditional and contemporary designs.

- **Oak** is a highly textured wood that's defined by a straight grain and natural elegance. It’s porous nature makes it extremely absorbent. Darker stains bring out its grain pattern, although light stains also work well with this wood. Like cherry, oak is extremely durable and long lasting.

- **Pine's** long grain and visible knots give it a rugged yet sophisticated look. If you're considering it, opt for the more durable yellow pine. Its lighter nature makes it a popular choice for contemporary kitchens.

- **Bamboo** isn't wood but rather a type of fast-growing grass. It is used mostly as a laminate material and has excellent anti-microbial properties and is quickly becoming and ecologically friendly choice. It is renewable and sustainable.


### Wood Veneer

Wood veneers cost anywhere from **$2,000 to $15,000 or more**. They are strips of wood applied to [plywood or particleboard](https://www.homeadvisor.com/cost/cabinets/#plywood). They offer the same look and feel of wood without the higher price tag. Also, some veneers are more resistant to humidity and moisture than traditional wood. You can stain them to match other decorative features of your kitchen.

### Stainless Steel Kitchen Cabinets Cost

Stainless steel runs **$25,000 to $38,000**. Because of the price and aesthetic, it’s a less common material than wood, laminate or veneer. Regardless, many homeowners are opting for stainless because of its durability, look and extremely long lifespan.

These types are recyclable and non-toxic, which works well if you want an environmentally-friendly kitchen. However, it's susceptible to scratches, finger prints and dents.

You’ll find stainless steel in commercial, modern and contemporary kitchens. When paired with darker woods, like cherry or oak, these cabinets fit with transitional and eclectic décor.

### Laminate

Laminates run **$4,000 to $15,000**. Like wood veneers, this is a cost-friendly alternative to wood. It’s a synthetic material that's adhered to particle board in both high-pressure and low-pressure procedures. High-pressure laminate is a higher quality product sold under brand names like Formica and Pionite.

It is highly durable and stands up to the wear and tear of a busy kitchen. Also, it doesn't warp or fade like some wooden alternatives. Laminate is especially at home in affordable, modern or contemporary kitchens.

### Acrylic Cabinets Prices

Acrylics range anywhere from **$5,000 to $20,000** or more depending on the base material used. Acrylic is a very hard, high-gloss finish applied as you would a varnish. It comes in various colors typically on flat panels for an ultra-modern look. Cost differences are due to the underlying material used, which can be MDF, wood, metal or others.

## Kitchen Cabinetry Estimates by Design

Cabinet design falls into two camps: framed and frameless. **On average, you’ll end up spending 15% more on frameless styles.** The cost of framed and frameless vary based on the wood, construction and size of your project. The most important decision when considering design is whether it will fit your existing décor. Here's a quick breakdown of each:

### Framed Cabinets

![framed-kitchen-cabinets](https://s3media.angieslist.com/s3fs-public/framed-kitchen-cabinets.jpeg?impolicy=infographic)

Framed cost **$5,000 to $20,000 or more.** A framed build means that the rails and stiles are visible at the front and form a flat frame that the door is attached to. They are best for homeowners seeking a traditional design.

### Frameless Cabinets

![frameless-cabinets](https://s3media.angieslist.com/s3fs-public/frameless-cabinets.jpeg?impolicy=infographic)

Frameless cabinets cost **$6,000 to $30,000 or more.** Custom designs lead to higher-end pricing as ranges are almost always due to the quality of the materials, level of customization and size of your kitchen. They are used in contemporary and modern designs. Frameless construction relies on a heavier box to hide the frame and hang the doors.

### Inset Cabinets

![insert-cabinets](https://s3media.angieslist.com/s3fs-public/insert-cabinets.jpeg?impolicy=infographic)

Inset, or flush, cabinets have the same ranges as most other semi-custom and custom or **$150 to $1,200 per linear foot.** Expect to pay about **20% more** for flush mounts than a similar traditional design with the same grade of materials. This type has a unique design where the drawers and doors, when closed, are flush with face frame openings.

### Cost of Shaker Cabinets

![shaker-cabinets-in-kitchen](https://s3media.angieslist.com/s3fs-public/shaker-cabinets-in-kitchen.jpeg?impolicy=infographic)

Shaker cabinets cost the same as any other style or between **$100 and $1,200 per linear foot.** This is a style choice for the doors and drawer faces, not a material difference. The type of material you use and whether you choose stock or custom will dictate the actual prices.

## Pricing Cabinets by Size

### 10x10 Kitchen Cabinets Cost

A 10 foot by 10 foot kitchen runs **$2,000 to $24,000,** depending on the type and materials you choose. This sized room typically has 20 to 25 linear feet of cabinetry.

### 12x12 Cabinetry

Cabinets for a standard 12 by 12-foot kitchen costs **$2,500 to $29,000**. This size is closer to the average kitchen size in US homes. The size of kitchens has doubled since the 1920s to accommodate open concepts, increased storage needs and larger and more numerous appliances.

### Cupboards for Small Kitchens

You’ll end up spending between **$1,700 to $20,000** on cupboards for a small kitchen **.** A small kitchen is typically 70 square feet or less. You don’t have to sacrifice style or efficiency in homes with smaller kitchens. You’ll find there are creative storage solutions in a wide range of materials and styles for all kitchen sizes.

### L-Shaped Kitchen Cupboards

![l-shaped-kitchen](https://s3media.angieslist.com/s3fs-public/l-shaped-kitchen.jpeg?impolicy=infographic)

The traditional L-shaped kitchen has two walls of counters and cabinets extending from one corner of the room and running along perpendicular walls.

## Additional Considerations

Not all cabinet installations are hang-and-go projects. Here are some additional considerations to include in your plan:

- **Moving Appliances:** Depending on the size and design, you may run into additional issues. For example: If your new layout requires you to move your oven, you'll need to relocate electrical, gas and ventilation systems as well. Your pro should spot any appliance-related issues before the job begins, but it's important to keep an eye out yourself.

- **Proper Measuring:** It's a good idea to hire a pro to take the measurements. Mis-measuring your space will result in job delays and expensive reinstallations.

- **Hiring the right pro:** As with any project, it's important that you hire the right pro at the right price.


Here are a few questions to help you choose the perfect professional for your project:

1. **How long have you been installing cabinets?**

2. **May I have a copy of your insurance and license?** The proper paperwork will safeguard your home and your pro in the event of an accident or faulty build.

3. **Do you have any references?** Always ask to speak to past clients before you hire a pro. Most trustworthy professionals are happy to provide a list of references. Avoid hiring a pro who hesitates or refuses to share his references or examples of past work.

4. **Do you offer material warranties?** It's nice to have a backup in case of an emergency. Ask your pro about material warranties for your project. If they don't offer a warranty, inquire about other guarantees.

5. **Will this project require a permit?** Always ask about [permit costs](https://www.homeadvisor.com/cost/architects-and-engineers/get-a-building-permit/) before you begin construction. Your pro should know about any licenses your project will require.

6. **How long will this job take?** Don't expect an exact completion date before you've hired your pro. But most pros can provide a rough estimate based on the layout of your home, the design of your cabinet installation and the company’s project schedule.


## FAQs

### What is the lowest-cost kitchen cabinets?

Stock cabinets are the lowest cost type at **$100 to $300 per linear foot.**

### What’s the cost of high-end kitchen cabinets?

Expect to pay **$13,000 to $30,000** for most high-end custom cabinets.

### How much is a bathroom vanity?

The [cost of a bathroom vanity](https://www.homeadvisor.com/cost/bathrooms/install-a-vanity/) runs anywhere from **$200 to $3,000**.

### How much are cabinets per square foot?

Kitchen cabinets aren’t sold per square foot, just per unit or when done professionally, by the linear foot. Expect to pay anywhere from **$100 to $1,200 per linear foot.**

### What material is best for white cabinets?

Any material works for white cabinets. There is no “best.” Choose the material that best fits with your budget and home value.

### What are the most popular cabinet brands?

Most popular brands are available across the United States at independent dealers. Most independent cabinet installers have access to a wide range of brand names. They can help you determine which one fits your budget and design.

- **Merillat Cabinetry**: A high-end brand that offers both the boxes and a wide range of hardware and accessories. Pulls and handles run from **$5 to $20**. You can find them from authorized dealers and any independent installer.

- **Starmark Cabinetry**: These handmade American cabinets manufactured in Sioux Falls, SD offer a wide range of design styles with custom options available. There are dealers located across the US in most urban centers.

- **Waypoint Living Spaces**: This high-end brand offers solid hardwood face frames found at numerous dealers in large metropolitan areas like Denver and San Francisco.

- **Wood-Mode**: Available in hundreds of locations across the United States, Canada and Central America. Speak with an installer for pricing. Brookhaven is one of their more well-known brand names.

- **Schuler** : Available exclusively at Lowes, these cabinets offer a wide range of designs materials and customization options for a middle of the road price.

- **Crystal Cabinets**: This popular independent custom manufacturer offers both affordable and premium options. You can find independent dealers in most urban areas.

- **MasterBrand Cabinets** : Available brands include **Decora, Homecrest, Kemer, KitchenCraft, Diamond, Aristokraft, Omega and Schrock.** An independent installer has access to all these brands and can direct you to the one that best fits your design and budgetary needs.

  - **Decora and Omega**: high-end flagship brands for large kitchens and budgets.

  - **Kemper, KitchenCraft, Homecrest and Diamond**: best for those with moderate budgets. Flexible customization options.

  - **Aristokraft** : for budget conscious homeowners.

# Discover Custom Cabinets Near Me for Your Home

by  \| Jun 9, 2026 \| [custom cabinets](https://fresnocabinets.com/category/custom-cabinets/)

![](https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1598928473162-0316ade00bbe?crop=entropy&cs=tinysrgb&fit=max&fm=jpg&ixid=M3w1MjQ0NjR8MHwxfHNlYXJjaHw2fHxjdXN0b20lMjBjYWJpbmV0c3xlbnwwfDB8fHwxNzgxMDMxODY5fDA&ixlib=rb-4.1.0&q=80&w=1080)

# Discover Custom Cabinets Near Me for Your Home

\_Okay, so you’re looking to get some custom cabinets for your place, huh? That’s a smart move if you want your kitchen, bathroom, or any other room to truly feel like _yours_. Basically, ‘custom cabinets near me’ means finding local woodworkers, cabinet makers, or specialized shops that can build exactly what you envision, rather than picking something off the shelf. It’s about getting cabinets that fit your space perfectly, match your style, and work for how you actually use your home.\_

Let’s be straight: off-the-shelf cabinets can be okay, but they often come with compromises. They might not fit that awkward corner perfectly, their depth might be a bit off, or the style might be _almost_ right but not quite. Custom cabinets are about eliminating those “almosts” and “nots quites.” They’re designed and built from the ground up to suit your specific needs and aesthetics. Think about it: a perfectly laid-out kitchen where every pot and pan has its place, or a bathroom vanity that maximizes storage in a small footprint. That’s the real beauty of going custom. You’re investing in functionality and a look that’s uniquely yours.

### The “Perfect Fit” Advantage

This is probably the biggest draw. Walls aren’t always straight, and neither are kitchen layouts after years of use. Standard cabinets come in fixed dimensions, and while you can get fillers and clever workarounds, they rarely achieve that seamless, built-in look. Custom cabinets, on the other hand, are measured precisely and built to fill every inch of your space. This means no wasted gaps, no awkward jutting out, and a much cleaner, more integrated appearance. It’s like tailoring clothes versus buying off the rack – one just fits better.

### Unlocking Your Style Potential

The world of cabinet finishes, materials, and hardware is vast. When you go custom, you get to play in that sandbox. You’re not limited to a few pre-selected wood types or paint colors. This is your chance to create a cohesive look that flows throughout your home. Want dark, moody walnut cabinets with clean, minimalist hardware? Or perhaps a bright, farmhouse-inspired shaker style with antique brass pulls? With custom, you can bring those specific design ideas to life.

### Built to Last, Built for You

Quality is often a major differentiator with custom cabinets. While mass-produced cabinets can vary wildly in their construction, custom builders typically use higher-grade materials and more robust joinery methods. They’re built with the intention of lasting for decades, not just a few years. Plus, you can specify features that make your daily life easier – think pull-out shelves for pots, specialized drawer dividers for spices, or deep drawers for appliances.

If you’re considering upgrading your kitchen or bathroom with custom cabinets, you might find it helpful to explore the process of cabinet refacing as an alternative. Refacing can provide a fresh look while saving time and money compared to a full remodel. For more information on this topic, check out the article on cabinet refacing at [Fresno Cabinets](https://fresnocabinets.com/refacing-cabinets/). This resource offers insights into the benefits and steps involved in cabinet refacing, making it a great complement to your search for custom cabinets near you.

## How to Find That Local Cabinet Maker

“Near me” is the key phrase here. You’re not just looking for any cabinet maker, but one that’s geographically convenient. This offers several advantages, from easier site visits and consultations to the potential for lower delivery costs and a more personal relationship with your builder.

### Leveraging Online Search Tools

Start with the obvious: your favorite search engine. Type in phrases like “custom kitchen cabinets \[your city/zip code\],” “local cabinet maker \[your town\],” or “bespoke cabinetry services near me.” Pay attention to the results. Do they have websites? Do the websites look professional and showcase high-quality work? Look for businesses that specialize specifically in cabinetry rather than general contractors who might subcontract the work out.

### Asking Around the Neighborhood

Word-of-mouth is still gold, especially for skilled trades. Talk to friends, family, neighbors, or even coworkers who have recently had renovations done. Did they use custom cabinets? Who did they use? Would they recommend them? Personal referrals often come with genuine insights into a builder’s reliability, communication, and the quality of their work.

### Exploring Local Design Centers and Showrooms

Many towns have design centers or showrooms that feature local artisans and manufacturers. Even if they don’t directly sell custom cabinets, they might have a curated list of recommended builders or showcase examples of high-end cabinetry that points you in the right direction. Visiting these places can also give you a sense of different styles and finishes that might inspire you.

### Checking with Architects and Interior Designers

If you’re working with a design professional, they are an invaluable resource. They often have established relationships with trusted custom cabinet makers they’ve worked with on previous projects. They know who is reliable, who delivers quality, and who can execute a specific design vision.

## What to Look For in a Custom Cabinetry Service

![](https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1639405069836-f82aa6dcb900?crop=entropy&cs=tinysrgb&fit=max&fm=jpg&ixid=M3w1MjQ0NjR8MHwxfHNlYXJjaHw4fHxjdXN0b20lMjBjYWJpbmV0c3xlbnwwfDB8fHwxNzgxMDMxODY5fDA&ixlib=rb-4.1.0&q=80&w=1080&w=900)

Once you’ve got a few potential names, it’s time to vet them. You want to feel confident that you’re hiring someone capable and trustworthy.

### A Strong Portfolio and Testimonials

This is your first clue to their capabilities. A good custom cabinet maker will have a portfolio—either online or in a physical brochure—showcasing their past projects. Look for a variety of styles and types of cabinetry. Do you like the aesthetic? Does the craftsmanship appear to be of high quality? Also, seek out testimonials or online reviews. What are previous clients saying about their experience? Look for consistent praise regarding communication, punctuality, and the final product.

### Clear Communication and Design Process

The process of designing and building custom cabinets can be complex. A good cabinet maker will have a clear, structured process, from initial consultation and design to material selection, fabrication, and installation. They should be excellent communicators, keeping you informed at every stage, answering your questions promptly, and being transparent about timelines and potential challenges.

### Material and Construction Knowledge

Don’t be afraid to ask about materials. What types of wood do they work with? What are the pros and cons of different finishes (e.g., lacquer, paint, stain)? How are the cabinets constructed (e.g., dovetail joints, full-extension drawer slides)? Their knowledge and willingness to explain these details will tell you a lot about their expertise and commitment to quality.

### Warranties and Guarantees

While not always explicitly stated, a good builder stands behind their work. Inquire about any warranties on their craftsmanship or the materials they use. This offers peace of mind and demonstrates their confidence in the longevity of their products.

## The Custom Cabinet Design Process: What to Expect

![](https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1604943963840-a18fe96a6322?crop=entropy&cs=tinysrgb&fit=max&fm=jpg&ixid=M3w1MjQ0NjR8MHwxfHNlYXJjaHw3fHxjdXN0b20lMjBjYWJpbmV0c3xlbnwwfDB8fHwxNzgxMDMxODY5fDA&ixlib=rb-4.1.0&q=80&w=1080&w=900)

Getting custom cabinets isn’t usually a quick, in-and-out process. It’s a collaboration that requires your input and the builder’s expertise. Understanding the typical steps can help you navigate it smoothly.

### The Initial Consultation and Site Visit

This is where it all begins. You’ll typically meet with the cabinet maker, either at their workshop, a showroom, or your home. This is your chance to discuss your needs, your vision, your budget, and your timeline. They’ll likely measure your space accurately and discuss potential design ideas based on the existing architecture and your preferences. Be prepared to share inspiration photos or design boards.

### Design Development and Material Selection

Based on the consultation, the cabinet maker will develop design concepts. This might involve sketches, 3D renderings, or detailed drawings. You’ll work together to refine these designs, making adjustments to layout, style, and features. This is also when you’ll dive deep into material selection—wood species, stain or paint colors, hardware, and any specialized interior organization solutions.

### Quotation and Contract

Once the design is finalized, you’ll receive a detailed quotation outlining the scope of work, materials, labor, and the total cost. Carefully review this document. It should clearly state what’s included, payment schedules, and estimated completion dates. A well-drafted contract is essential for protecting both parties.

### Fabrication and Production

With the contract signed and a deposit paid, the cabinet maker will begin fabricating your cabinets. This is the period when your vision starts to take physical form in their workshop. Depending on the complexity and the builder’s workload, this can take several weeks to a few months.

### Installation

This is the exciting final stage. The craftsman will visit your home to install the cabinets. This requires precision and care to ensure they are perfectly aligned, securely fastened, and that all doors and drawers operate smoothly. It’s a good idea to be present during installation to address any last-minute questions or minor adjustments.

If you’re considering upgrading your kitchen or living space, exploring options for custom cabinets near you can be a great way to enhance both functionality and aesthetics. A related article that delves into the benefits of tailored cabinetry is available at [Maximizing Space with Custom Cabinets for Your Home](https://fresnocabinets.com/maximizing-space-custom-cabinets-for-your-home/). This resource provides valuable insights on how custom solutions can help you make the most of your available space while reflecting your personal style.

## Planning Your Budget for Custom Cabinets

| Location | Number of Custom Cabinet Shops | Average Customer Rating | Distance from My Location |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Downtown | 12 | 4.5 | 2 miles |
| Suburb A | 8 | 4.2 | 5 miles |
| Suburb B | 6 | 4.0 | 8 miles |

Custom cabinets are an investment, and understanding your budget from the outset is crucial for a smooth experience. It’s not just about the sticker price; it’s about getting the best value for your money.

### Understanding What Drives Cost

Several factors influence the final price tag of custom cabinets. The complexity of the design, the type of wood you choose (hardwoods like cherry or walnut are generally more expensive than others), the finish (specialty paints or multi-layer finishes cost more), the hardware (high-end hinges and drawer slides add up), and any custom interior organizers all play a role. The size of the project, of course, is also a major determinant.

### Getting Multiple Quotes (Wisely)

It’s always a good idea to get quotes from a few different reputable custom cabinet makers. However, be sure you are comparing apples to apples. Provide each potential builder with the exact same specifications and design ideas so you can accurately assess the pricing. Don’t just go for the cheapest option; consider the quality of materials, craftsmanship, and the builder’s reputation alongside the price.

### Prioritizing Your Needs

You might have a long wish list, but your budget might not accommodate it all. Work with your cabinet maker to prioritize what’s most important. Perhaps a stunning custom hutch is a must-have, but you can opt for a more standard pull-out for pots to save money. Or maybe you’d rather invest in superior drawer construction and a simpler door style to start. Identify your “must-haves” versus your “nice-to-haves.”

### The Long-Term Value of Custom

While the upfront cost of custom cabinets can be higher than stock options, consider the long-term value. Custom cabinets are built to last, potentially outliving other elements of your home renovation. They also significantly enhance the functionality and aesthetics of your space, which can contribute to your home’s overall appeal and value. It’s an investment in something that will serve you well for years to come.

\_Finding custom cabinets near you is all about connecting with local craftspeople who can translate your vision into reality. It’s a process that requires a bit of research and clear communication, but the result—a beautifully crafted, perfectly fitting set of cabinets that truly reflects your style—is well worth the effort. Happy hunting!\_

web.markdown:
# Custom Cabinets in St Paul, MN

We build custom cabinets for St Paul homes, then deliver and install them across the city and the east metro. Loon Cabinetry is a shop in Minneapolis, and most of our work sits a short drive away in places like Highland Park, Mac-Groveland, Crocus Hill, Como, and St. Anthony Park. If you’ve been searching for custom cabinets near me and keep landing on big-box showrooms or out-of-state factories, that’s the gap we fill. Everything we make is drawn, built, finished, and fitted by the same team, for one kitchen, one mudroom, one office at a time.

[Set Appointment](https://looncabinetry.com/request-quote/)

## What We Build for St Paul Homes

If you’re looking for a custom cabinet maker in St Paul, MN, here’s what we do. We design and build cabinets to fit your room, your storage habits, and the way you actually live, then we finish and install them ourselves. Kitchens are the bulk of our work, but we also build mudroom benches and lockers, home office built-ins, pantries, bathroom vanities, range hoods, and wet bars. Everything is made to order in our Minneapolis shop and installed across St Paul and the Twin Cities. No stock boxes pulled off a warehouse shelf, and no design work handed off to a third party who never sees your home.

![Custom Kitchen Cabinets](https://looncabinetry.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Custom-Kitchen-Cabinetry-Minneapolis.webp)

### Custom Kitchen Cabinets

Custom kitchen cabinetry made to fit your room instead of the other way around. Whether you want custom kitchen cabinets in St Paul, MN for a full remodel or a single run of uppers, we draw it to your space and your habits.

[Learn More ⟶​](https://looncabinetry.com/custom-kitchen-cabinetry-minneapolis/)

![Custom Mudroom cabinets](https://looncabinetry.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Custom-Mudroom-cabinets.webp)

### Mudroom Built Ins

Mudroom built-ins, mud benches, and lockers that hold up to St Paul winters and the boots, bags, and coats that come with them. Custom mudrooms sized to your entry, not a stock cabinet width.

[Learn More ⟶​](https://looncabinetry.com/custom-cabinets-for-mudrooms-minneapolis/)

![Home Office Built Ins Cabinets](https://looncabinetry.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Custom-Home-Office-Built-In.webp)

### Home Office Built Ins

Custom home office built-ins and cabinets for the spare room, the basement nook, or the corner you actually work from. Built-in desks, file storage, and shelving that look like furniture, not an afterthought.

[Learn More ⟶​](https://looncabinetry.com/custom-home-office-built-in-minneapolis/)

![Living Room Custom Cabinetry](https://looncabinetry.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Living-Room-Cabinetry.webp)

### Living Room Cabinets

Living room cabinetry built around your TV, your books, and the awkward chimney chase a lot of older St Paul houses have. Media walls, fireplace surrounds, and shelving that fit the room you already have.

![Custom Cabinets](https://looncabinetry.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Storage-Solutions.webp)

### Custom Pantry Cabinets

Pantry cabinets, floating shelves, and glass-front storage for the way you actually shop and cook. Pull-outs, deep drawers, and a spot for the small appliances that usually clutter the counter.

![Custom Bathroom Vanities](https://looncabinetry.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Custom-Bathroom-Vanities.webp)

### Custom Bathroom Vanities

Custom vanities sized to tight St Paul bathrooms, including the half-baths tucked under a staircase. Real wood boxes and finishes that can take steam and daily use without swelling or peeling.

![Wood range hood and custom cabinets in a St Paul kitchen](https://looncabinetry.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Custom-Range-Hood.webp)

### Custom Range Hood

A wood range hood built to match your cabinets and carry the eye up. We scale it to your ceiling height and venting so it reads as part of the kitchen, not a box bolted to the wall.

![Built-in Appliances](https://looncabinetry.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Built-in-Appliances.webp)

### Built-in Appliances

Panel-ready fronts and cabinetry that wrap your fridge, dishwasher, and microwave so the kitchen reads as one clean line. We plan the openings around your exact appliance specs before we build.

![Custom Wet Bar Cabinets](https://looncabinetry.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/Wet-Bar-Cabinets.webp)

### Wet Bar Cabinets

Wet bar and beverage cabinets for the basement, the dining room, or the spot you host from. Glass storage, a small sink run, and lighting worked in from the start.

## About Loon Cabinetry

![Alexei Ceban, owner of Loon Cabinetry, in the shop](https://looncabinetry.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/About-us-1024x847.webp)

Loon Cabinetry is a cabinet shop, not a cabinet store. We don’t sell boxes off a wall. We design, build, finish, and install custom cabinets for one client at a time, and St Paul is one of the areas we work in most.

A lot of what we build here goes into older houses. St Paul has a deep stock of 1900s-to-1930s homes, the bungalows in Como and Hamline-Midway, the bigger Victorians and Tudors around Summit Hill and Crocus Hill, the four-squares all over Mac-Groveland. They’re beautiful, and they’re rarely square. Walls bow, floors slope, and plaster does what plaster does. Stock cabinets fight that. Custom cabinets work with it. We measure the room as it actually exists, then build to those numbers, so the finished run sits tight to the wall instead of leaving gaps you fill with trim.

The other half of the job is taste. Some clients want a quiet Shaker kitchen that looks like it’s always been there. Others want flat-slab doors and a hidden pantry. We’re happy either way. What we care about is that the cabinets fit the house and fit how you live in it.

## Meet The Maker

Loon Cabinetry is owned and run by Alexei Ceban. Alexei is on every project, from the first measure-up in your St Paul home to the final reveal, and he builds and finishes alongside the team rather than handing it off. That’s the main reason people pick a small shop over a showroom: you talk to the person making your cabinets, not a salesperson reading a catalog. You can see current and finished work on our Instagram at [https://www.instagram.com/looncabinetry](https://www.instagram.com/looncabinetry).

## We Design, Build, Finish & Install — No Middlemen, Just Seamless Service.

[Set Appointment](https://looncabinetry.com/request-quote/)

**EXCELLENT**

Based on **40 reviews**

Posted on Google

Dan Haugdahl

Trustindex verifies that the original source of the review is Google.

Loon Cabinetry built 6 white oak floating shelves and a white oak bench cap for us, and all are fantastic quality and look beautiful. The craftsmanship is impressive. Communication was good and everything was completed on time. Highly recommend.

Posted on Google

## Why St Paul Homeowners Hire Us

![Custom home office built-ins by Loon Cabinetry](https://looncabinetry.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Why-Choose-us-1024x682.jpg)

A few things make the job easier and the result better when you work with one shop start to finish:

- One team, one point of contact. The same people draw it, build it, finish it, and install it. Nothing gets lost in a handoff between a designer and a factory.
- We handle the whole remodel when you want that. With trusted trade partners, we can coordinate plumbing, electrical, flooring, and tile so your kitchen project isn’t five separate phone calls.

- Lighting planned in, not added on. Under-cabinet and in-cabinet LED gets designed into the layout from the start, with the wiring planned before we build.

- Honest pricing. Because design and build happen under one roof, you skip the markup that stacks up when a store outsources the drawings to someone else.


## How We Build

![Custom kitchen cabinets installed in a St Paul, MN home](https://looncabinetry.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/loon-cabinetry-1-1024x863.webp)

The parts you don’t see are the parts that decide how long a kitchen lasts.

- **Furniture-grade plywood boxes**. We build cabinet boxes from furniture-grade plywood, not particle board. It holds screws better, shrugs off the odd leak, and won’t sag under a stone countertop.

- **Real wood drawers**. Drawer boxes are solid wood, dovetailed, on soft-close runners. It’s a small thing you’ll notice every day for twenty years.

- **Soft-close everywhere**. Doors and drawers all close quietly on their own. No slamming, no kids’ fingers.

- **Finishes built to last**. We spray professional-grade finishes that stand up to steam, grease, and cleaning, whether it’s a full kitchen or a single vanity.


When it comes to custom cabinets, we handle it all: **design, build, finishing and installation**. By working directly with us, you avoid the potential cost inflation that can occur when design services are outsourced separately.

Let Loon Cabinetry transform your kitchen into the heart of your home. Contact us today for a consultation and experience the Loon difference.

## Working With Local Cabinet Makers in St Paul

There’s a real difference between buying st paul cabinets off a showroom floor and hiring cabinet makers who build for your house. A showroom sells you the closest stock size and trims out the rest. We start with your room and your measurements. For a lot of St Paul kitchens that means dealing with a chimney chase in the corner, a window that sits too low for standard uppers, or a radiator we have to build around. Stock can’t solve those. A custom kitchen design can.

If you’ve searched for cabinet makers near me or a custom built-ins contractor in St Paul, MN and felt like everyone either sells cheap flat-packs or quotes commercial-job prices, we’re the middle option a lot of people are looking for: a small shop that does residential custom work at a fair price, with the owner on the tools.

We take on full custom kitchen designs in St Paul, MN as well as smaller pieces, a mudroom bench here, an office wall of built-ins there. No job is too small to do right, and none is too big to keep personal.

**EXCELLENT**

## Custom Cabinet Questions From St Paul Homeowners

### Where can I find good cabinet makers near me in St Paul?

Loon Cabinetry builds for St Paul from our shop in Minneapolis, usually fifteen to twenty-five minutes from your door. We come out, measure, design, build, and install, so “near me” actually means the same people the whole way through. We work across Highland Park, Mac-Groveland, Summit Hill, Como, St. Anthony Park, and the surrounding neighborhoods.

### How much do custom cabinets cost in St Paul, MN?

It depends on the size of the room, the door style, and the materials, so the honest answer is that we quote each project after we see it. A single mudroom bench is a different number than a full kitchen with an island and a pantry. We give you a detailed design and a written estimate before you commit to anything, and there’s no charge for the first consultation.

### Do you build mud benches and custom mudrooms near me?

Yes. Mudroom built-ins are one of the most-requested jobs we do for St Paul homes, partly because the winters are hard on entryways. We build mud benches, lockers, hooks, and closed storage sized to your space, with finishes that handle wet boots and salt. If you’ve been looking for mud benches near me or custom mudrooms in St Paul, MN, that’s squarely in our wheelhouse.

### Can you build custom home office built-ins?

Yes. Custom home office built-ins and home office cabinets are a big part of what we make now. Built-in desks, file drawers, shelving, and cabinet runs designed for the room you actually work in, whether that’s a spare bedroom, a basement, or a corner of the living room.

### Why choose custom kitchen cabinets over stock?

Stock cabinets come in fixed widths and assume your walls are straight. Most St Paul houses aren’t. [Custom kitchen cabinets](https://looncabinetry.com/custom-kitchen-cabinetry-minneapolis/) are built to your room’s real measurements, so you don’t lose space to filler strips, and you get to choose the layout, the wood, and the finish. You also get drawers and storage planned around how you cook, not around what fit in the box.

### Do you do the whole remodel or just the cabinets?

Either one. We can build and install just the cabinetry, or we can coordinate the full kitchen remodel with trusted plumbers, electricians, and tile and flooring crews. One point of contact for the project keeps it from turning into a juggling act for you.

### How long does a custom cabinet project take?

Most kitchens run a few weeks in the shop once the design is signed off, plus install time. Smaller pieces like a mudroom bench or an office built-in are quicker. We give you a real timeline with your estimate so you can plan around it.

# Custom Kitchen Cabinet Companies

## St Petersburg, FL

Licensed and Fully Insured

Elegant Designs

### Upgrade Your Kitchen With Custom Cabinets Designed for Your Lifestyle

[Custom kitchen cabinet companies in St Petersburg, FL](https://www.ortegacustomcabinets.com/custom-cabinet-services)turn your cooking space into a functional, beautiful environment tailored specifically to your needs. At Ortega Custom Cabinets, we design and build kitchen cabinets that maximize your space while reflecting your personal style.

Our cabinet solutions are crafted in-house by skilled artisans who value quality construction and attention to detail. We offer a wide range of custom cabinet options to enhance your kitchen:

- Full custom cabinet designs
- Premium wood selection including maple, cherry, oak, and walnut
- Specialized storage solutions
- Island cabinets with integrated features
- Pull-out pantry systems
- Custom drawer configurations
- Decorative molding and trim options
- Integrated lighting systems
- Soft-close hardware
- Glass-front display cabinets

Your kitchen deserves cabinets that are as unique as your home. [Reach out to us](https://www.ortegacustomcabinets.com/contact)today to discuss your custom kitchen cabinet project. When comparing custom kitchen cabinet companies in St Petersburg, FL, homeowners consistently choose our personalized approach and unmatched craftsmanship. We take pride in elevating everyday cooking areas into stunning culinary retreats.

### Custom Cabinets Create Functional Kitchen Spaces

ustom kitchen cabinet companies in St Petersburg, FL provide solutions that prefabricated options simply cannot match. When considering a kitchen renovation or new construction, understanding the advantages of custom cabinetry helps you make informed decisions about your investment. Here's why custom kitchen cabinets might be the right choice for your home:

- Maximization of awkward or unusual kitchen spaces
- Perfect fit for non-standard ceiling heights
- Solutions for support beams or angled walls
- Storage designed for your cooking habits
- Integration with existing architectural features
- Space for specialized appliances
- Improved workflow through strategic placement
- Enhanced property value through built-in features
- Personalized aesthetic that reflects your taste
- Durability beyond mass-produced alternatives

Custom cabinets address unique kitchen challenges while providing superior functionality. Our design team assesses your space, listens to your needs, and creates cabinet solutions that work perfectly for your lifestyle and home configuration.

### Comprehensive Design and Materials

Our design process begins with a careful evaluation of your current layout to uncover hidden potential. We explore various aesthetics with you through detailed 3D renderings. Many custom kitchen cabinet companies in St Petersburg, FL outsource conceptual work, but we handle everything locally. This close collaboration guarantees the final installation matches your initial dream perfectly. Furthermore, living in a coastal region requires materials that handle high humidity. When selecting wood, you want options that withstand these factors, which is a priority for leading custom kitchen cabinet companies in St Petersburg, FL. We source premium plywoods and kiln-dried hardwoods designed to resist warping over time.

﻿

### Elevating Your Entire Home Experience

The kitchen is the centerpiece of family gatherings, holiday celebrations, and quiet morning routines. Upgrading this vital room does more than improve storage; it fundamentally changes how you interact with your house. A well-organized, aesthetically pleasing cooking environment encourages healthier meals at home and provides a welcoming atmosphere. Finding the right fit for your home improvement journey is essential when evaluating custom kitchen cabinet companies in St Petersburg, FL. We are committed to fostering joy and pride in your living environment. Beyond simply hanging wooden boxes, we are crafting the beautiful backdrop for your family's most cherished memories.

﻿

### Our Seamless Installation Process

A flawless design requires a flawless installation. Once your custom pieces are crafted in our local workshop, our dedicated team of certified carpenters takes over to bring the vision to life in your home. When comparing custom kitchen cabinet companies in St Petersburg, FL, homeowners consistently appreciate our commitment to a pristine, stress-free installation experience. We understand that a kitchen renovation can temporarily disrupt your daily routine, which is why we prioritize efficiency, cleanliness, and clear communication from start to finish.

- **Respect for Your Home:**We use protective coverings to safeguard your floors, walls, and existing architectural features while we work.
- **Precision Fitting:**Our installers meticulously adjust every hinge, track, and molding to ensure doors hang perfectly straight and drawers glide with zero resistance.
- **Clean Workspaces:**We perform daily cleanups and ensure your new kitchen is absolutely spotless upon final completion.
- **Final Walkthrough:**We never consider a job finished until we walk through the completed space with you to ensure every detail meets our rigorous standards and your exact specifications.

Our meticulous attention to detail during this final phase guarantees that the transition from a 3D rendering to your physical reality is smooth, seamless, and entirely spectacular.

### Why Choose Ortega Custom Cabinets

Selecting the right cabinet maker for your kitchen project is crucial to achieving results that meet your expectations and provide lasting value. Ortega Custom Cabinets stands apart from other cabinet providers through our commitment to quality, service, and customer satisfaction.

- $250 off on cabinet installation for new customers
- Licensed and fully insured for your protection
- Functional designs that enhance daily kitchen use
- Quality materials that ensure longevity
- Over 20 years of industry experience
- Minority business accelerator certification
- Nominated twice for Small Business of the Year from Tampa Bay Chamber
- 2023 Business of the Year from Prospera USA
- Certified carpenter on our professional team
- In business since 2010 with proven reliability
- Locally and family-owned with deep community roots
- Woman and minority-owned business
- All products built in-house and installed by our team

Our approach combines traditional craftsmanship with modern design principles. By maintaining control over the entire process-from initial consultation through design, manufacturing, and installation-we ensure consistent quality and accountability at every stage of your cabinet project.

### Contact Us

Your dream kitchen begins with thoughtfully designed custom cabinets that reflect your style and meet your functional needs. Our design consultants are ready to discuss your vision and provide guidance on options that will work best in your space.

Create your ideal kitchen with custom cabinets designed and built specifically for your home. [Contact us](https://www.ortegacustomcabinets.com/contact)to schedule a consultation and begin your journey toward a more beautiful, functional kitchen.

![Ortega Custom Cabinets](https://le-cdn.hibuwebsites.com/38b0e98ef83b46eba28a06c47bc24ad0/dms3rep/multi/opt/icon-callout-coupon-ortega-custom-cabinets-1920w.jpg)

Save $250 On Installation

Transform your home with beautiful custom cabinets and enjoy $250 off our professional installation service throughout Clearwater, Tampa, Bradenton, and St. Petersburg areas.

Contact Ortega Custom Cabinets today to claim your savings!

metadata.description: 1 review of WHOLESALE CABINET SUPPLY "The cabinet quality is fantastic.  All the usual bells and whistles such as slow closing doors, high quality wood, etc.  My 40 cabinet purchase was done based upon the prior excellent reviews on other sites.  All was done on good faith, since I was too lazy to visit the showroom.  The finished product was excellent.  The owner, Scott, personally assisted with helping resolve my mistake.  Very happy with the decision to use them and have referred others to them after they saw the finished product."

web.markdown:
[Skip to content](https://knowseafood.com/top-phoenix-rta-cabinet-suppliers-for-high-volume-renovation-work/#content)

# Top Phoenix RTA Cabinet Suppliers for High-Volume Renovation Work

![Line 12](https://knowseafood.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/Line-12.png)

[![](https://knowseafood.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/Mason-Bennett-768x768.png)](https://knowseafood.com/author/mason/)

- [Mason Bennett](https://knowseafood.com/author/mason/)

- [On June 26, 2026](https://knowseafood.com/2026/06/26/)

- [Kitchen & Home Essentials](https://knowseafood.com/category/kitchen-home-essentials/)

![Construction worker installing cabinets in a sunlit kitchen with mountain view through windows](https://knowseafood.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/top-phoenix-rta-cabinet-suppliers-for-high-volume-renovation-work.webp)

## Table of Contents

01. [Why Top Phoenix RTA Cabinet Suppliers Are Worth a Closer Look](https://knowseafood.com/top-phoenix-rta-cabinet-suppliers-for-high-volume-renovation-work/#why-top-phoenix-rta-cabinet-suppliers-are-worth-a-closer-look)

02. [Comparing the 5 Top Phoenix RTA Cabinet Suppliers](https://knowseafood.com/top-phoenix-rta-cabinet-suppliers-for-high-volume-renovation-work/#comparing-the-5-top-phoenix-rta-cabinet-suppliers)

03. [Highland Cabinetry – Best for Wholesale Cabinet Supply for Contractors and Homeowners](https://knowseafood.com/top-phoenix-rta-cabinet-suppliers-for-high-volume-renovation-work/#highland-cabinetry-best-for-wholesale-cabinet-supply-for-contractors-and-homeowners)

04. [PA Kitchen – Best for Custom Cabinet Manufacturing and Whole-Home Customization](https://knowseafood.com/top-phoenix-rta-cabinet-suppliers-for-high-volume-renovation-work/#pa-kitchen-best-for-custom-cabinet-manufacturing-and-whole-home-customization)

05. [Kavalan Cabinetry – Best for Wholesale Cabinet Supply for Builders and Designers](https://knowseafood.com/top-phoenix-rta-cabinet-suppliers-for-high-volume-renovation-work/#kavalan-cabinetry-best-for-wholesale-cabinet-supply-for-builders-and-designers)

06. [DL Cabinetry – Best for Kitchen and Residential Cabinetry Manufacturing](https://knowseafood.com/top-phoenix-rta-cabinet-suppliers-for-high-volume-renovation-work/#dl-cabinetry-best-for-kitchen-and-residential-cabinetry-manufacturing)

07. [ROC Cabinetry – Best for Wholesale Cabinet Supply for Contractors and Dealers](https://knowseafood.com/top-phoenix-rta-cabinet-suppliers-for-high-volume-renovation-work/#roc-cabinetry-best-for-wholesale-cabinet-supply-for-contractors-and-dealers)

08. [Methodology Behind These Picks](https://knowseafood.com/top-phoenix-rta-cabinet-suppliers-for-high-volume-renovation-work/#methodology-behind-these-picks)

09. [Picking the Right Top Phoenix RTA Cabinet Suppliers for You](https://knowseafood.com/top-phoenix-rta-cabinet-suppliers-for-high-volume-renovation-work/#picking-the-right-top-phoenix-rta-cabinet-suppliers-for-you)

10. [The Verdict](https://knowseafood.com/top-phoenix-rta-cabinet-suppliers-for-high-volume-renovation-work/#the-verdict)


When you’re sourcing cabinets for a high-volume Phoenix remodel, the wrong supplier doesn’t just slow you down. It can cost you the whole job. The top Phoenix RTA cabinet suppliers handle the pressures that matter most here: dry desert heat that warps particleboard boxes, tight project timelines that can’t wait weeks for national shipping, and bulk orders where plywood construction quality is non-negotiable. After reviewing supplier data, customer feedback, and real-world delivery performance across multiple vendors, this guide breaks down the five best options worth your time.

**How this ranking was put together**

Public data formed the backbone of this research. Each company was assessed using review platform feedback, case study evidence, product documentation, and directory listings. Only suppliers with a verified track record in furniture manufacturing and cabinet distribution made the cut.

_→ See the full research breakdown_

- **Highland Cabinetry** – Best for wholesale cabinet supply for contractors and homeowners
- **PA Kitchen** – Best for custom cabinet manufacturing and whole-home customization
- **Kavalan Cabinetry** – Best for wholesale cabinet supply for builders, contractors, and designers
- **DL Cabinetry** – Best for kitchen and residential cabinetry manufacturing
- **ROC Cabinetry** – Best for wholesale cabinet supply for contractors and dealers

## Why Top Phoenix RTA Cabinet Suppliers Are Worth a Closer Look

Phoenix’s climate creates specific problems that suppliers in cooler, more humid regions simply don’t face the same way. Low humidity and intense heat put stress on cabinet boxes fast, and particleboard construction will show that stress sooner than you’d like. Beyond material quality, Phoenix contractors deal with inventory gaps that push lead times from days into weeks, which kills project momentum on tight schedules.

Choosing a well-matched supplier here isn’t just about price. The right fit means lower cost per linear foot of installed cabinetry without trading away box construction grade. It means more plywood and less particleboard in the boxes that actually matter. And shorter assembly time per cabinet unit, because hardware quality holds up, and instructions are clear.

That kind of consistency is rare. And it shows directly in your project outcomes.

## Comparing the 5 Top Phoenix RTA Cabinet Suppliers

Note: All data in this table is sourced from review platforms and the official websites of the listed companies.

| **Company Name** | **Years Operating** | **Team Size** | **Headquartered In** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Highland Cabinetry | Since 2018 | 85 employees | Phoenix, Arizona |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| PA Kitchen | Since 2009 | 350+ sales; 300+ installers | Foshan, China |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Kavalan Cabinetry | Since 2018 | Not disclosed | Austin, TX |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| DL Cabinetry | Since 2006 | 51-200 | Jacksonville, Florida |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ROC Cabinetry | Since 2014 | 40+ | Norcross, Georgia |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |

## Highland Cabinetry – Best for Wholesale Cabinet Supply for Contractors and Homeowners

![Modern kitchen interior with white cabinetry, stainless steel appliances, and barstools at marble island countertop](https://knowseafood.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/highland-cabinetry-wholesale-cabinets-phoenix.webp)

**How Does Highland Cabinetry Operate?**

Highland Cabinetry manufactures all-wood cabinets in Vietnam and ships them nationwide, with contractors ordering [wholesale cabinets in Phoenix](https://highlandcabinetry.com/wholesale-cabinets/arizona/phoenix/) benefiting from delivery windows as short as four days via freight. They carry over 200,000 cabinets in stock and produce more than 25,000 kitchen sets monthly. That kind of inventory scale is rare at this price point. Their dedicated online portal makes order management straightforward, and contractor financing partnerships mean larger orders don’t require full upfront capital. They also back everything with a lifetime warranty.

**Why Does Highland Cabinetry Stand Out as a Top Phoenix RTA Cabinet Supplier?**

For Phoenix contractors dealing with long national shipping delays and heat-sensitive particleboard failures, Highland Cabinetry’s all-wood construction and four-day freight option directly address the two biggest sourcing headaches in this market. The combination of lifetime warranty coverage, deep inventory, and vertical manufacturing control puts them in a different category from most national wholesale options. Honestly, it’s hard to find that full package elsewhere at this price.

**What Users Are Actually Saying:**

Customers consistently call out pricing as a major draw, especially compared to what local showrooms charge for similar quality. The consistent feedback pattern points to strong product quality relative to price, with delivery speed mentioned as a genuine differentiator. That kind of reliability across both quality and timing is what keeps contractors coming back for repeat orders.

## PA Kitchen – Best for Custom Cabinet Manufacturing and Whole-Home Customization

![Modern kitchen interior with marble countertops, red cabinets, and high stools in sleek showroom setting](https://knowseafood.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/pa-kitchen-custom-cabinet-manufacturer.webp)

**How Does PA Kitchen Operate?**

PA Kitchen, now operating under the PA Home brand, covers five full product categories: cabinets, doors, windows, wall panels, and bathroom systems. Founded in 2009 and headquartered in Foshan, China, they bring Industry 4.0 automated production lines with a reported 0.1% error rate and 200,000 annual unit capacity. Their partnerships with hardware suppliers like Blum and Hettich signal a genuine commitment to quality at the component level. With over 42,000 completed global projects and 3,500+ B2B clients, they carry serious weight in the custom manufacturing space.

**Why Does PA Kitchen Stand Out as a Top Phoenix RTA Cabinet Supplier?**

PA Kitchen solves the custom specification problem that most RTA suppliers can’t touch, offering whole-home product coordination across cabinets, walls, and bathroom systems under one roof. For developers or builders running multi-room renovation projects where finish consistency matters across every surface, that breadth of scope is hard to match.

**What Users Are Actually Saying:**

PA Kitchen doesn’t have a large public review footprint in the traditional sense, but their B2B client count and project volume tell a consistent story. Contractors and developers working at scale tend to stay loyal once they’ve completed a project, which points to reliable execution rather than one-time wins.

## Kavalan Cabinetry – Best for Wholesale Cabinet Supply for Builders and Designers

![Modern kitchen with gray cabinets, stainless steel appliances, and white marble countertops with lemons](https://knowseafood.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/kavalan-cabinetry-wholesale-all-wood-cabinets.webp)

**How Does Kavalan Cabinetry Operate?**

Kavalan Cabinetry runs out of a 65,000 square foot warehouse and showroom in Austin, Texas. They manufacture semi-custom and custom all-wood cabinets using maple wood and plywood exclusively, with no compressed woods or particleboard in the mix. They own their manufacturing facility directly, which cuts out middleman costs and keeps testing and standards in-house. CARB compliance certification adds environmental credibility that matters on commercial and multi-family projects. And they’ve built serious regional standing in Texas in under seven years, which is genuinely impressive.

**Why Does Kavalan Cabinetry Stand Out for Top Phoenix RTA Cabinet Suppliers?**

Kavalan’s direct manufacturing ownership means they control box construction grade from the source, which cuts out the quality inconsistency that shows up when suppliers are just reselling someone else’s product. For builders and designers who need plywood-only construction without negotiating for it, that non-negotiable material standard is a real advantage.

**What Users Are Actually Saying:**

Public reviews for Kavalan are limited, but their growth across the Texas market speaks to a consistent client experience. Builders who prioritize material transparency and CARB-certified sourcing tend to find Kavalan’s approach worth the slightly longer shipping distance from Austin to Phoenix-area projects.

## DL Cabinetry – Best for Kitchen and Residential Cabinetry Manufacturing

![Modern kitchen with white cabinets, stainless steel appliances, and marble countertops in bright lighting](https://knowseafood.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/dl-cabinetry-residential-kitchen-cabinets.webp)

**How Does DL Cabinetry Operate?**

DL Cabinetry has been manufacturing semi-custom residential and multifamily cabinets since 2006, operating out of Jacksonville, Florida, with multiple Southeast locations. Every cabinet ships with Grade-A plywood boxes, solid wood frames, dovetail joints, and hydraulic soft-close hardware as standard (not as upgrades, which is worth calling out). They manufacture and distribute their own products, so pricing stays closer to wholesale even on premium specs. Free design services round out the offering for contractors who need layout support alongside product supply.

**Why Does DL Cabinetry Stand Out as a Top Phoenix RTA Cabinet Supplier?**

DL Cabinetry solves the spec inconsistency problem that plagues mid-range cabinet purchasing by building dovetail joints and soft-close hardware into every unit by default, rather than charging extra for them. Their track record on large-scale orders, including furnishing a 300+ room university dormitory with solid maple cabinets, shows they can handle volume without quality drift.

**What Users Are Actually Saying:**

The Home Builders Association of Greater New Orleans awarded DL Cabinetry with a 2013 Talk of the Town Louisiana Award, which carries more weight than most online review snippets. Contractors working on residential and multifamily projects appreciate the predictable construction quality and the fact that design support is included rather than billed separately.

## ROC Cabinetry – Best for Wholesale Cabinet Supply for Contractors and Dealers

![Cabinet samples with discount tags on beige patterned background promoting clearance sale](https://knowseafood.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/roc-cabinetry-wholesale-contractor-supplier.webp)

**How Does ROC Cabinetry Operate?**

ROC Cabinetry has operated as a wholesale cabinet manufacturer and supplier since 2014, covering Florida and Georgia from a multi-location warehouse network. They offer 27 distinct cabinet products across series, including Shaker, Inset, Escada, Newtown, Belmont, Classic, and Euro Deluxe, in both framed and frameless options. What’s interesting about their setup is the live inventory syncing built through their e-commerce partnership with Kitchen365, which means dealers and contractors see real-time stock levels before placing orders. That removes a lot of the guesswork from high-volume purchasing.

**Why Does ROC Cabinetry Stand Out as a Top Phoenix RTA Cabinet Supplier?**

ROC addresses the ordering friction that slows down contractors managing multiple concurrent projects through live inventory visibility and a modern e-commerce setup that most cabinet suppliers still haven’t built. Their diverse product range across 27 cabinet options means dealers can source different style families from a single supplier without splitting orders.

**What Users Are Actually Saying:**

ROC Cabinetry’s reputation in the Southeast leans heavily on customer service and pricing consistency. Dealer and contractor clients point to personalized design support and inventory reliability as the main reasons they keep returning, which suggests ROC has invested in the relationship side of wholesale distribution rather than just the transactional side.

## Methodology Behind These Picks

### Gathering Your Source Data

The research process started broadly. A longlist was built by pulling supplier profiles from industry directories, trade-focused review platforms, and cabinet manufacturer databases. Company websites were reviewed for product specifications, construction details, and service descriptions. Case studies and project portfolios were collected that were publicly available to get a clearer picture of what each supplier actually delivers at scale.

### The Shortlist Cut

From that initial pool, options with missing verification, thin review histories, or no documented presence in the furniture manufacturing space were removed. Review patterns were analyzed across multiple sources rather than taken at face value from a single platform. Suppliers where customer sentiment was inconsistent or where negative patterns repeated across independent sources were filtered out. Only companies with a documented track record of delivering on the specific demands of cabinet supply, whether wholesale, semi-custom, or RTA, moved forward.

### Fact-Checking the Picks

Each shortlisted company’s stated claims were cross-referenced against publicly available evidence. Production capacity figures, delivery time windows, material specifications, and construction standards were checked against what customers and contractors actually reported in real-world project reviews. Where company marketing language conflicted with observed performance, the real-world data carried more weight. This step is where several initially promising options got removed because the claims didn’t hold up under scrutiny.

### Authority Signals and Industry Standing

Beyond reviews and product specs, attention was paid to broader authority indicators. Industry awards, mentions in trade publications, and certifications from established manufacturing bodies were factored in. Companies that had built a verified standing through professional associations or had been cited in project-specific contexts carried more credibility than those relying purely on self-generated marketing content. Award mentions and third-party citations signal that a supplier’s reputation extends beyond their own website.

### Top Phoenix RTA Cabinet Suppliers’ Track Record

Finally, each company was assessed for its relevance to the Phoenix and Southwest market context. This meant looking at dedicated service coverage, verified reviews from contractors working in comparable climates, and case study evidence showing performance on projects similar to high-volume Phoenix renovation work. Suppliers with Phoenix-specific landing pages, regional inventory positioning, or documented project history in hot, low-humidity climates received higher consideration because they’ve already shown they understand the local sourcing environment.

## Picking the Right Top Phoenix RTA Cabinet Suppliers for You

Choosing between these five suppliers comes down to your specific project context. A high-volume flipper in Phoenix has different priorities than a custom builder working on a luxury kitchen remodel, and the right match depends on what matters most to your workflow. Here are the factors to work through before committing to a supplier.

- **Industry and Domain Experience:** Look at how long the supplier has been operating in cabinet manufacturing and whether they have documented project history in climates similar to Phoenix’s dry heat. Experience with warping-prone conditions matters more than general tenure.
- **Features and Service:** Consider whether you need free design support, a live inventory portal, damage replacement policies, or contractor financing. Some suppliers bundle these in; others charge extra or don’t offer them at all.
- **Pricing Structure:** Compare cost per linear foot of installed cabinetry across suppliers, not just sticker price per box. Factor in shipping costs to Phoenix, assembly difficulty, and whether soft-close hardware and plywood construction are included as standard.
- **Results Measurement:** Look for warranty length on the box, finish, and hardware, alongside the door and drawer cycle rating. A longer warranty and higher cycle rating signal real confidence in build quality.
- **Industry Knowledge and Compliance:** For commercial or multi-family projects in Phoenix, CARB Phase 2 formaldehyde emission standards and ANSI/KCMA A161.1 certification matter. Verify these before bulk purchasing, especially for projects subject to HOA material requirements or code compliance for multi-family housing.

## The Verdict

Phoenix renovation contractors need suppliers who understand heat, volume, and tight timelines. Plywood box construction, fast freight options, and real warranty backing are what separate a reliable RTA source from one that costs you the project. Highland Cabinetry leads this list because of its inventory depth, four-day delivery capability, and lifetime warranty. As Phoenix’s renovation market keeps growing, suppliers built for scale and speed are positioned to stay ahead.
